ideation:
This sequence will depict a light, delicate arm movement to emulate the fluttering of a butterfly. The arms will move in a gentle, synchronized pattern, with the wrists playing a key role in mimicking the butterfly's wings.  The movement will be slow and graceful, avoiding any abrupt changes in position. To maintain a balance between expressiveness and natural movement, we will use the examples of waving and celebratory gestures as inspiration. 
In the first frame, the arms are at the sides, neutral and relaxed. This sets the stage for the gentle movement that will follow. Frame two shows a slight upward movement, with the wrists turning slightly outward to start the 'flutter'. Frame three will showcase the peak of the flutter, with the arms lifted higher and the wrists rotated more outward. Frame four will reflect the downward motion, gently lowering the arms while maintaining the wrist rotation. Finally, frame five returns both arms to the resting position.

frames.beat: arms hang relaxed at the sides, still air
arms lift slightly, wrists turn out gently
arms rise higher, wings begin to flutter
arms descend, a gentle downward flutter
arms return to rest, the butterfly lands
